content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


March 4, 2004


Diana D. Parnell, M.D.
Marin Health Care District
1100 South Eliseo Drive, Suite 4
Greenbrae, CA 94904

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-04-027

Dear Dr. Parnell:

This letter is in response to your request for advice on the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a) (copy enclosed.)) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor and confers immunity under the Act. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice from you or private counsel.




1. Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109 – 18996, of the California Code of Regulations.

Advice File No. W-04-027
Page No. 2

Should you have any questions, please contact the Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

William J. Dankeit
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
WJL:jg

sections.conclusion: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.
sections.question_synthetic: Dr. Parnell requested written advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the California Political Reform Act concerning her duties as an official.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same or very similar issues are currently under investigation by the Enforcement Division, and such advice would be inappropriate at this time.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter with no formal question or conclusion sections. The document explicitly states the Commission declines to provide written advice and cites a pending investigation as the reason. No specific factual question or legal analysis provided.

QUESTION: Dr. Parnell requested written advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions under the California Political Reform Act concerning her duties as an official.

CONCLUSION: After a careful review of the facts you provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.
